reasoning: kewa:pamiehmena:n follows the pattern where kewa: marks 'I' as subject and the suffix combination indicates 'them' as object
'we see them' uses newa: for 'we' as subject and -ehmaki for 'them' as object
'they see me' has no subject prefix but uses -iehm for 'me' as object and -naki to mark 'they' as subject
ne- appears as part of newa: which marks 'we' as subject
-eθ appears in all second person forms
-i appears between the root and certain suffixes as a linking element
